title: Translational Risk Rubric
description: Schema and scoring rubric for computing translational risk ratings across model organism / human pathway component comparisons. Risk is derived from five weighted dimensions: sequence conservation, paralog complexity, regulatory context, phenotypic validity, and therapeutic evidence.

risk_level.description: Final risk rating for translational validity.

rubric_metadata.description: Provenance and versioning for the rubric itself.

dimensions.description: The five scoring dimensions. Each has a weight, scale, criteria levels, and guidance notes.

D1_sequence_conservation.description: How similar is the model organism protein to the human protein at the sequence and structural level?

weight.description: 30% of total risk score. Foundational but not sufficient alone.

5.label: Very High
5.criteria: ≥90% protein identity across full length; all functional domains present and conserved; key active-site residues identical.

4.label: High
4.criteria: 70–89% identity; all major functional domains present; minor divergence in regulatory regions or loops.

3.label: Moderate
3.criteria: 45–69% identity; core functional domain conserved; diverged N/C termini or regulatory motifs; may affect post-translational modification.

2.label: Low
2.criteria: 20–44% identity; functional domain recognisable but diverged; ortholog assignment may rely on synteny or functional data rather than sequence alone.

1.label: Very Low
1.criteria: <20% identity; structural homology only (e.g. predicted by fold recognition); functional equivalence requires experimental validation.

0.label: Absent
0.criteria: No identifiable ortholog or structural homolog. Functional analog may exist by convergent evolution only.

properties.auto_score_rule: Map conservation_score (0–1 float from pathways.json) to level: ≥0.90→5, ≥0.70→4, ≥0.45→3, ≥0.20→2, >0.00→1, 0.00→0
caveats: High sequence identity does not guarantee functional equivalence if key regulatory motifs differ.
Low identity does not preclude functional conservation — especially for IDRs and scaffold proteins.
Assess domain-by-domain when the protein has multiple independent functional units.

D2_paralog_complexity.description: How does gene family size and redundancy in the model organism compare to human? More paralogs = more compensation = harder to model loss-of-function.

weight.description: 20% of total. Paralog divergence is a major source of translational failure.

5.label: Equivalent
5.criteria: Same number of paralogs in model and human; expression and functional domains equivalent; no known compensation between paralogs.

4.label: Minor mismatch
4.criteria: ±1 paralog difference; any missing paralog has non-overlapping expression pattern or tissue distribution; compensation unlikely.

3.label: Moderate mismatch
3.criteria: 2–3 paralog difference; some functional overlap between paralogs known; model may under- or over-represent pathway activity.

2.label: Major mismatch
2.criteria: Human has substantially more paralogs (≥3 extra); model organism paralog cannot distinguish isoform-specific biology; tissue specificity likely to differ.

1.label: Single vs family
1.criteria: Model has 1 gene where human has a large family (≥4 members) with distinct tissue, developmental, or disease roles. Model findings may reflect average or dominant paralog only.

0.label: No homolog
0.criteria: No paralog or functional analog exists in model organism for a human gene family member critical to the pathway.

properties.auto_score_rule: Compare n_model_paralogs to n_human_paralogs. Apply compensation evidence as modifier: +1 if no compensation known, -1 if compensation documented.
caveats: Fewer paralogs in model (e.g. fly) can be an advantage — cleaner phenotype — but cannot model isoform-specific drug targets.
In mouse, paralogs may compensate during development, masking phenotypes seen in human cancer where compensation is lost.
Whole-genome duplication in zebrafish means it often has MORE paralogs than human — assess subfunctionalisation.

D3_regulatory_context.description: Are the upstream regulators, downstream effectors, post-translational modifications, and tissue expression contexts conserved between model and human?

weight.description: 20% of total. Regulatory divergence frequently explains translational failures that sequence conservation cannot predict.

upstream_inputs.description: Are the activating signals and upstream kinases/ligands conserved?

downstream_outputs.description: Do the same target genes respond downstream?

ptm_conservation.description: Are key phosphorylation, ubiquitination, SUMOylation sites present?

tissue_expression.description: Is the gene expressed in the same tissue/cell types relevant to the disease model?

5.label: Fully conserved
5.criteria: All upstream and downstream pathway connections conserved; PTM sites identical; tissue expression equivalent; no known rewiring.

4.label: Largely conserved
4.criteria: Core regulatory connections conserved; ≤1 minor PTM site differs; tissue expression broadly equivalent with minor differences.

3.label: Partially conserved
3.criteria: Major regulatory connections present but some rewiring documented; key PTM site absent or relocated; tissue expression partially overlapping.

2.label: Substantially diverged
2.criteria: Upstream or downstream connections substantially different; important PTM sites absent; model gene expressed in different tissues than human disease-relevant tissue.

1.label: Largely diverged
1.criteria: Regulatory context mostly non-equivalent; model uses different upstream activators and/or drives different target genes; expression restricted to non-disease-relevant tissues.

0.label: Completely diverged
0.criteria: No meaningful regulatory conservation. Gene may be expressed but embedded in a fundamentally different pathway context.

caveats: TGF-β → p15^INK4b is a mammalian-specific regulatory input absent in fly — would score 1 for fly, 5 for mouse.
Tissue expression data should be drawn from the disease-relevant cell type, not whole-organism RNA-seq.
Regulatory rewiring can be context-dependent: conserved in one tissue type but diverged in another.

D4_phenotypic_validity.description: Has the model organism been experimentally shown to recapitulate the human phenotype when the gene is manipulated? Direct experimental evidence that the model is valid.

weight.description: 20% of total. Experimental phenotypic evidence is the strongest validation of translational relevance.

5.label: Fully validated
5.criteria: Loss/gain-of-function in model produces equivalent phenotype to human disease; human gene rescues model phenotype; drug response concordant in ≥2 independent studies.

4.label: Substantially validated
4.criteria: Model recapitulates key disease phenotype; human gene rescue demonstrated OR drug response concordance shown; minor phenotypic differences noted.

3.label: Partially validated
3.criteria: Some phenotypic overlap with human disease; model published but with caveats (e.g. different tumour spectrum, developmental vs adult phenotype); rescue not tested.

2.label: Limited validation
2.criteria: Model gene manipulated but phenotype only partially mirrors human disease; significant differences in penetrance, expressivity, or tissue affected.

1.label: Predicted only
1.criteria: No published disease model; phenotypic equivalence inferred from pathway membership or sequence homology alone.

0.label: Contradicted
0.criteria: Experimental evidence shows model does NOT recapitulate human phenotype, or manipulation produces opposite effect.

caveats: Rb1 KO mouse is embryonic lethal — phenotypically diverges from human RB1 loss despite high sequence conservation. Scores 3, not 5.
Distinguish between developmental and somatic disease models — a developmental phenotype may not predict adult cancer biology.
Drug response concordance in a model is the strongest evidence; concordance with ≥2 approved drugs scores maximum.

D5_therapeutic_evidence.description: Is there direct evidence that drugs or interventions targeting this component in the model organism predict human therapeutic outcomes?

weight.description: 10% of total. Therapeutic concordance is the ultimate translational test but often not yet available.

5.label: Clinical concordance
5.criteria: Component is an approved drug target; model organism drug response predicts human clinical outcomes; biomarker validated in both model and clinic.

4.label: Strong preclinical
4.criteria: Drug active in model with mechanism confirmed; compound in clinical trials; model-derived biomarker under clinical investigation.

3.label: Preclinical evidence
3.criteria: Drug active in model; mechanism plausible but not confirmed; no clinical data yet or clinical outcome mixed.

2.label: Limited evidence
2.criteria: Drug active in model but mechanism unclear; or known discordance between model and human drug response for this target.

1.label: No drug data
1.criteria: No pharmacological data available for this component in this model organism.

0.label: Discordant
0.criteria: Drug active in model but known to fail in humans for this target; or human drug inactive in model.

caveats: CDK4/6 inhibitors (palbociclib) only work in RB1-intact tumours — a model lacking p16 but with intact Rb1 may falsely predict sensitivity.
Drug concordance established for one species pair does not automatically transfer to other species comparisons.
Absence of drug data (score 1) is not evidence of failure — it simply means this dimension cannot yet be evaluated.

aggregation.description: Rules for combining dimension scores into a final composite risk score.

method.description: Primary method: weighted sum of normalised dimension scores. Override rules apply for critical gaps.

formula.description: Composite score ranges 0.0 (worst) to 1.0 (best). Thresholds map composite to risk level.

normalisation.const: Each dimension score (0–5) is divided by 5 before weighting, giving a 0–1 contribution per dimension.

override_rules.description: Hard override rules that can elevate risk regardless of composite score. Applied after weighted sum.

default.rationale: No homolog means the component cannot be modelled at all in this species. Composite score is meaningless.
Experimental evidence of non-equivalence overrides any positive sequence or regulatory scores.
Known drug discordance between model and human is a direct translational failure signal.
No homolog AND low sequence similarity: the component is functionally absent in the model.
Both regulatory context and phenotypic evidence point to non-equivalence — elevate regardless of sequence.

modifier_rules.description: Optional modifiers that adjust composite score before threshold mapping.

default.condition: Human gene rescue of model phenotype demonstrated (D4 evidence_type = rescue)
default.rationale: Human gene rescue is the gold standard of functional conservation. Bonus applied.

default.condition: Drug response concordance validated in ≥2 independent studies
default.rationale: Replicated pharmacological concordance is strong translational validation.

default.condition: Known tissue-specific expression mismatch for disease-relevant cell type
default.rationale: Even conserved genes may not be expressed in the model tissue equivalent to the human disease tissue.

default.condition: Whole genome duplication in model (zebrafish) creates functionally distinct paralogs
default.rationale: Zebrafish WGD paralogs are often subfunctionalised — one paralog may not cover the full human gene function.

default.condition: Evidence of convergent evolution (different mechanism, same phenotype)
default.rationale: Convergent phenotype can mislead. Drug mechanism in model may not apply to human even if phenotype matches.

final_risk_thresholds.description: Maps composite score (after modifiers, before overrides) to a final risk level. Overrides are applied last.

low.label: Low Risk
low.action: Model organism findings are likely to translate directly. Proceed with confidence; document assumptions.

moderate.label: Moderate Risk
moderate.action: Translate with caution. Identify the specific divergent dimension(s) and design experiments to bridge the gap before clinical development.

high.label: High Risk
high.action: Significant translational gap. Findings from this model should not be extrapolated to humans without orthogonal validation in a higher-fidelity model or human cell system.

critical.label: Critical Gap
critical.action: Do not extrapolate. This component cannot be validly modelled in this organism. Use an alternative model or human-derived system (organoids, iPSCs, patient samples).

flags.description: Boolean flags that annotate the output record with interpretive warnings. Applied independently of composite score.

no_homolog.message: No ortholog identified in model organism. Component cannot be directly studied.

paralog_compensation_risk.message: Paralog compensation documented. Loss-of-function phenotype in model may be masked or exaggerated relative to human.

tissue_expression_mismatch.message: Gene not expressed in model organism tissue equivalent to human disease tissue. In vivo phenotype may not be informative.

ptm_site_absent.message: One or more key post-translational modification sites absent in model organism. Phosphorylation-dependent or ubiquitin-dependent drug mechanisms may not apply.

regulatory_rewiring.message: Documented rewiring of upstream or downstream pathway connections. Drug acting on regulatory inputs may have different efficacy profile.

developmental_vs_somatic_mismatch.message: Model phenotype is developmental; human disease is somatic (or vice versa). Findings may not transfer to adult disease context.

drug_discordance.message: Known discordance between drug response in model and human. Do not use this model for therapeutic screening of this target.

wgd_paralog_risk.message: Zebrafish whole-genome duplication has produced extra paralogs. Assess subfunctionalisation before assuming equivalence.

output_schema.description: Schema for the structured risk assessment record produced by the scoring engine for each component × species pair.

dimension_rationales.description: Free-text justification for each dimension score.

action.description: Recommended researcher action given the final risk level.

flags_raised.description: List of flag IDs triggered for this assessment.

summary_narrative.description: Auto-generated or human-written plain-language summary of the risk assessment for use in educational reports.
